title: Async & Await ⏱️
description: Learn the magic words to pause time without freezing the app.

slides.title: Opening the Box 🎁
slides.content:
# The `await` Keyword

So how do we open the `Future<String>`? We command Dart to `await` it!

```dart
// Place 'await' directly before the Future task.
String realData = await downloadFromInternet();
```
`await` tells Dart: *"Pause THIS specific function until the box opens, extract the real String, and put it into my variable."*
Crucially, it ONLY pauses this function. The rest of the app keeps running flawlessly!

slides.title: The Rule of Await 📜

quizData.question: What is the relationship between `async` and `await`?

options.text: They mean the exact same thing.
You can only use `await` inside a function that has been marked as `async`.
You can use `await` anywhere, but `async` makes it faster.

quizData.explanation: You cannot await a Future unless the surrounding function is flagged as async.

slides.title: Infectious Futures 🦠

quizData.question:
Fill in the blank to pause execution until the login finishes: 
bool success = _____ api.login();
quizData.explanation: The 'await' keyword unlocks the Future block and prevents moving to the next line until data returns.

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: Watch how we simulate a 2-second delay using Dart's built-in `Future.delayed`!
